tense: A confrontation nearby is about to spill over — NPC pushes another, voices rising.
Someone is watching the exits. They leave the moment your character looks at them.
A weapon or dangerous object is visible where it shouldn't be.
celebratory: The crowd is too loud; something important is being drowned out.
An NPC is very drunk and says something they shouldn't.
The festivities are cover for something being moved or exchanged.
deserted: The emptiness has a reason. Signs of a hasty departure.
A single light on in a room that should be empty.
Something has been left behind that wasn't meant to be found.
chaotic: Your character is separated from the point of interest by the crowd or commotion.
Emergency services are on their way. Time is short.
An NPC mistakes your character for someone else — dangerously so.
mournful: Someone is grieving openly and blocks access to the area you need.
The site is connected to a recent death. Residents are reluctant to speak.
A memorial or shrine has appeared overnight. Nobody knows who placed it.
conspiratorial: A meeting is occurring that wasn't meant to be observed.
Someone passes a note or object with careful casualness.
An NPC goes suddenly silent and gives your character a look.
predatory: Another Kindred is here. They haven't noticed you yet.
A mortal is being slowly isolated from the group by someone.
The hunting ground is marked. Someone has been here recently.
oblivious: The mortals here are completely unaware of the supernatural element in plain sight.
Your character's careful approach was unnecessary — nobody is paying attention.
An opportunity exists to act openly. But doing so may attract attention later.
generic: A Resident has witnessed more than intended. They will remember.
Something at the site has changed since you last heard about it — for the worse.
An unexpected figure appears: someone who shouldn't be here.
Equipment, access, or an NPC that was available is now unavailable.
The scene ends abruptly. Something has spooked the site.
